title: Implement connection pool and embedded/remote deployment modes
purpose: Deliver the two deployment modes of the Rust SDK -- embedded (zero-hop, in-process) and remote (wire protocol) -- behind the same trait API, per ADR-010's embedded-first architecture. Also deliver configurable connection pooling for remote mode multi-channel workloads. After this brief lands, a Rust developer can switch between embedded and remote by changing configuration, not code (story S2).
task: Create embedded.rs, remote.rs, and connection/pool.rs. The embedded module provides concrete ChannelHandle and ConversationHandle implementations that delegate directly to in-process liminal channel handles with no serialisation or network overhead. The remote module provides implementations that use the wire protocol transport with the connection lifecycle and recovery from SDK-003. Both modules implement the same traits defined in SDK-002. The connection pool manages multiple connections for remote mode workloads. IN SCOPE: embedded mode, remote mode, connection pooling, same-trait API for both modes. OUT OF SCOPE: Gleam SDK, TypeScript SDK, cross-SDK conformance.

requirements.title: Implement embedded mode with direct in-process channel handles
requirements.spec: WHEN the SDK is configured in embedded mode, THE SYSTEM SHALL provide ChannelHandle and ConversationHandle implementations that interact with liminal channels through direct in-process handles. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT perform serialisation, protocol framing, or network I/O in embedded mode. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require a running liminal server for embedded operation.
acceptance: EmbeddedChannelHandle implements the ChannelHandle trait from SDK-002
EmbeddedConversationHandle implements the ConversationHandle trait from SDK-002
Publish in embedded mode does not invoke any serialisation (message is passed by reference or move)
No network socket is opened in embedded mode
Embedded mode can be instantiated with only an liminal crate reference, no server address

requirements.title: Implement remote mode with wire protocol transport
requirements.spec: WHEN the SDK is configured in remote mode, THE SYSTEM SHALL provide ChannelHandle and ConversationHandle implementations that communicate over the liminal wire protocol (ADR-008). THE SYSTEM SHALL use the connection lifecycle state machine from SDK-003 for connection management. THE SYSTEM SHALL use subscription recovery from SDK-003 on reconnect. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose protocol frames or wire format details to the application.
acceptance: RemoteChannelHandle implements the ChannelHandle trait from SDK-002
RemoteConversationHandle implements the ConversationHandle trait from SDK-002
Remote mode requires a server address in its configuration
Remote mode uses ConnectionState from SDK-003 for lifecycle management
Remote mode uses SubscriptionRecovery from SDK-003 on reconnect
No wire protocol types appear in the public API of RemoteChannelHandle or RemoteConversationHandle

requirements.title: Ensure same API trait for both embedded and remote modes
requirements.spec: WHEN application code is written against the ChannelHandle and ConversationHandle traits, THE SYSTEM SHALL allow switching between embedded and remote mode without changing application code. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a builder or configuration struct that selects the mode and returns the appropriate concrete type behind the trait.
acceptance: A single function or builder constructs either embedded or remote handles based on configuration
Application code using dyn ChannelHandle or impl ChannelHandle works with both modes
Switching from embedded to remote requires only changing the SdkConfig, not the application code
The builder returns Result<impl ChannelHandle, SdkError> regardless of mode

requirements.title: Implement configurable connection pool for remote mode
requirements.spec: WHEN the SDK operates in remote mode with multiple channel subscriptions, THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a connection pool managing a configurable number of connections. THE SYSTEM SHALL accept pool size configuration from the caller. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT impose a hardcoded default pool size -- the default is deferred to the builder or runtime.
acceptance: ConnectionPool struct manages a configurable number of connections
Pool size is set via ConnectionPoolConfig with a max_connections field
No hardcoded default pool size exists in the source code
Multiple channel subscriptions can share pooled connections
Pool distributes subscriptions across available connections

boundaries: SHALL NOT expose deployment mode differences to application code beyond the configuration step.
SHALL NOT implement Gleam or TypeScript SDK logic.
SHALL NOT impose hardcoded connection pool limits or default sizes.
SHALL NOT modify the ChannelHandle or ConversationHandle trait signatures from SDK-002.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal-sdk -- -D warnings passes with no diagnostics
cargo fmt -p liminal-sdk --check reports no formatting issues
Unit tests verify embedded mode publish/subscribe without network
Unit tests verify remote mode uses the connection lifecycle state machine
Unit tests verify connection pool distributes subscriptions across connections
Integration test demonstrates switching from embedded to remote by changing config only
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
